  "findings": [
    "Aluminum conductor terminated on a device not rated CO/ALR",
    "Potential fire hazard due to galvanic corrosion and thermal expansion mismatch"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace the non-compliant device with one rated for CO/ALR",
    "Alternatively, pig-tail the aluminum wire to a copper conductor using an approved splicing connector before terminating on the device"
  ],
  "rationale": "Terminating aluminum conductors on devices not specifically listed and marked CO/ALR (or AL/CU for larger sizes) is a direct violation of electrical safety standards. It creates a high risk of overheating, oxidation, and electrical fires due to the differing thermal expansion rates and galvanic action between the aluminum wire and the device terminals.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14",
    "NEC 404.14(C)",
    "NEC 406.3(C)"
  ]
}
